summary: Executed backend API regression tests and frontend UI/integration checks for Fab Luxe landing page. Backend lead CRUD is stable; frontend has a reproducible desktop sticky CTA popup failure.

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking [data-testid="sticky-cta-button"] did not open lead popup in repeated automation runs (dialog did not become visible).

integration_issues.issue: Popup visibility check timed out after click (3 attempts). Header, hero, and final CTA open popup correctly.

issues: Third-party 'Made with Emergent' badge occupies same area where sticky CTA is expected, likely causing visibility/clickability conflict.

action_items: Fix desktop sticky CTA interaction/visibility so it reliably opens lead dialog.
After fix, retest desktop sticky CTA + mobile sticky CTA popup flow and confirm lead submit success state through UI.
Optionally add analytics/test hook around CTA click to detect blocked click events.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend lead endpoints correctly exclude MongoDB _id from responses and return proper 404/400 behavior for not-found and empty update payload.
Frontend uses process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL (good), but CTA interaction reliability issue remains for desktop sticky CTA.
seed_data_creation: Created temporary TEST_ leads via API tests; cleaned during test teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite: 6/6 passed. UI automation confirmed landing load, price text, and popup opening from header/hero/final CTA. Repeated timeout occurred specifically when validating sticky desktop CTA opening dialog; stopped after 3 failed attempts per loop-prevention rule.
rca of the issue: Repro: open page on desktop -> click sticky CTA -> wait for lead dialog. Expected: dialog opens. Actual: dialog not visible within timeout. Probable cause: sticky CTA display/click obstruction in bottom-right area (possible overlap/conflict with injected badge or stacking/z-index interaction). Mitigation: inspect computed styles/z-index/pointer-events for sticky CTA and any fixed overlays at bottom-right, then retest.
